give and take reply from 9b: Test if the 9B model can successfully generate a response to a simple prompt.
chek why: Investigate why a system setting changed automatically or something failed.
bro: A friendly term. Respond in a casual, highly confident, and direct tone without apologies.
do it: Execute the technical solution immediately without asking for permission or explaining too much.
ords: Means 'words'. Do not correct the spelling, just understand the intent.
imliment: Means 'implement'. Write the code to build the feature immediately.
gem: An extremely advanced, hidden, or optimal technical solution that most people overlook.
shrimp attack: Refers to Latch-Mediated Spring Actuation (LaMSA). Storing low compute power over time to release it in one massive, explosive output burst.
